message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I'm implementing StreamListener debug/trace logging (opt-in debug flag + guarded DEBUG logs) entirely within streaming_listener.py. Let me know your feature's files so we can confirm disjoint ownership.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class). If your feature (is_last_chunk) needs to touch streaming_listener.py too, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions - I'll only touch __init__, receive(), and add a new tick() method. Please tell me which parts of streaming_listener.py you need so we can split cleanly.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Same file, both features confined to it - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential edits to avoid clobbering. My change is small and purely additive: (1) new __init__ params idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts, (2) one line in receive() to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk, (3) a brand-new tick() method appended after flush(). I won't touch your debug-log lines. I'll go first since it's a tiny diff, then send DONE - please hold off editing until you see my DONE, then add your guarded debug logs (feel free to sprinkle them anywhere, including inside receive(), since I'm only adding one new line there near the top for the timestamp refresh). Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Ack - we share this file, I don't need messages.py at all (no StreamResponse schema changes). My feature (debug/trace logging) touches: __init__ (add debug: bool=False, debug_logger param + self._logger/self.debug init - I'll append these as the LAST params after yours), the reset block at top of receive() (add one guarded log line when allow_reuse clears state - won't change logic, just adds a log call), start-detection branches, the rolling-end-check block, the emit-chunk return point (add log calls only, no logic changes), and flush() (add log call only). To avoid collision: please finish your idle_timeout_s/tick() edits first and send DONE - I'll then re-read the file and layer my guarded logging calls in around your logic without touching your added code/branches. I will not modify any behavior, only add  guarded log lines plus the debug/debug_logger params. OK to proceed in that order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - proceeding now with idle_timeout_s/tick() additive changes. Will send DONE when finished, then it's your turn.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - you go first with idle_timeout_s/tick(), send DONE when finished, then I will add my guarded debug-log lines around your code without touching your additions.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts tracking in __init__, one refresh line in receive() right after chunk_message is confirmed non-None ('if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()'), and a new tick() method appended right after flush() (before find_predictor_for_stream_listeners). Tests pass (tests/streaming/ -k listener). Your turn to layer in debug/trace logging - feel free to touch any existing line for guarded log calls, just please don't remove/alter the idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() logic itself.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished layering guarded debug/trace logging on top of your changes: added debug/debug_logger params + self._logger init in __init__, a _debug_preview() truncation helper, and guarded DEBUG log calls at start-detection (both cache-hit and queue-accumulation branches), rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush(), and reset. No behavior changes - only added log calls plus two small variable extractions (post_identifier_buffer, truncated_buffer) that preserve identical return values. Did not touch your idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() logic. Tests pass (tests/streaming/). Patch written to patch.txt.
